metadata.title: Dynamic Head Selection for Efficient Attention
metadata.abstract: We propose a dynamic head selection mechanism that reduces attention FLOPs by 29% while improving accuracy to 87.4%.

research_problem.context: Attention mechanisms are core to modern deep learning.
research_problem.practical_problem: Standard attention scales quadratically with sequence length.
research_problem.technical_problem: Reducing attention complexity without losing accuracy.
research_problem.existing_gap: Existing sparse attention methods lose accuracy on hard examples.
research_problem.research_question: Can dynamic head selection reduce FLOPs while preserving accuracy?

contributions.statement: A novel dynamic head selection mechanism for attention.
contributions.novelty: First to use learned gating for attention head selection.

method_overview: Dynamic head selection learns a gating function that filters inactive attention heads.

method_components.name: Gating Module
method_components.purpose: Select active attention heads per input.
method_components.mechanism: Learned lightweight relevance score with threshold.

findings.claim: Dynamic head selection improves accuracy by 3.9 points over Baseline B.
Dynamic head selection reduces FLOPs by 29% compared to Baseline A.

limitations.statement: Dynamic head selection introduces additional hyperparameters requiring per-dataset tuning.
limitations.implication: May require additional compute budget for hyperparameter search.

limitations.statement: Evaluated only on image classification and machine translation.
limitations.implication: Results may not generalize to other domains.

evidence.source_text: We propose a novel approach to attention mechanisms that improves accuracy.
We introduce grouped-query attention with dynamic head selection.
Our method achieves 87.4% top-1 accuracy, outperforming Baseline B by 3.9 points while using 29% fewer FLOPs.
Dynamic head selection introduces additional hyperparameters that require tuning per dataset. We only evaluate on image classification and machine translation.
